_meta.purpose: Brand-name -> generic-name dictionary for the compound crosswalk. Whel's compound list contains entries written as brand strings ('Wellbutrin') or as 'Brand (generic)' parentheticals. The MATRIX drug-list keys by generic / DrugBank ID, so brand strings need an explicit translation step before crosswalking. This file lists every brand-to-generic mapping the audit script knows about, with provenance. Surfaced on /about/external-references so users can see exactly which translations the audit applies.
_meta.scope_note: Intentionally finite. Each entry is a real compound that appeared as a brand string in Whel's data and was missed by the name-only crosswalk. New entries should be added only when a brand string fails to match and the underlying generic IS in MATRIX. Do NOT add entries for class labels, supplements, or experimental compounds -- those are handled by the is_class_label / is_non_drug flags in scripts/check-matrix-coverage.py.

entries.note: FDA-approved 1985; antidepressant repurposed for smoking cessation. Brand widely used as shorthand.
FDA-approved May 2023 for moderate-to-severe vasomotor symptoms in menopause. NK3R antagonist.
SNRI; relevant to chronic pelvic pain repurposing.
Guanylate cyclase-C agonist; appears in endometriosis-adjacent GI repurposing.
Benzodiazepine; rectal suppository formulation appears in pelvic-floor dysfunction protocols.
Brand for the LNG-IUS. Whel's compound list separately enumerates the IUS as a class umbrella; this entry covers the brand-only mentions.
Injectable contraceptive; appears in heavy-menstrual-bleeding and endometriosis literature.
Contraceptive implant; relevant to menstrual-pattern repurposing.
Pelvic-floor injection use in chronic pelvic pain. Whel's existing onabotulinumtoxinA entry is matched; this covers the brand-only row.
GLP-1 agonist; appears in PCOS and metabolic repurposing. Generic semaglutide is already matched in the crosswalk; this entry exists for transparency about which brand strings the audit recognises.
Antihistamine; appears in mast-cell-activation repurposing for endometriosis.

Combo product for uterine fibroids and endometriosis pain; this entry matches on relugolix as the GnRH antagonist component. Estradiol and norethindrone components are handled separately in the SINGLE candidate list.

Medical-device combo product, not a drug in DrugBank. Listed here for documentation only -- this entry will NOT match a DrugBank ID and the underlying compound remains in the experimental / device category.
